title: On load, the Themes sidebar lists My themes and a built-in theme catalog with swatch preview chips, and the three-panel workspace (themes | editor | preview) is visible at a wide viewport
After creating a custom theme named 'Harbor Dawn' with valid required fields (hold-to-add or New), the text 'Harbor Dawn' is visible under My themes
After adding 3 new custom themes on top of any existing custom set, My themes shows exactly 3 more rows than before those creates
After changing a semantic color or radius control on the active custom theme, the live preview re-themes to match the new tokens without reload
After editing a custom theme name to 'Evening Tide', the text 'Evening Tide' is visible in My themes and the previous name is not
After deleting/removing a custom theme, that theme name is no longer visible under My themes
After removing all custom themes, My themes shows an empty state while built-in catalog themes remain available
After submitting create with an empty required theme name, the custom themes count does not increase and validation feedback is visible
After switching from Components Demo to Color Palette (or Component Variants) preview tab, the preview pane swaps dense demos without leaving the page
After switching focus between Themes list selection and Editor/Generator controls, both modes remain usable and the active theme stays in sync across panels
After clicking Random on a custom (or forked) theme, editor color tokens and the live preview change
When hovering theme rows, chrome controls, swatch chips, or primary buttons, a visible hover wash, tilt, or fill/border transition appears
